title: lavandula angustifolia subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Lavandula angustifolia subsp. pyrenaica, also known as Pyrenean lavender, is a subspecies of the narrow-leaved lavender plant, native to the Pyrenees mountain range. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Pyrenean lavender include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety and stress, and relief from pain and inflammation, making it a popular herbal remedy for promoting relaxation and overall well-being. Pyrenean lavender is often used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, teas, and topical creams, allowing for various ways to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
